For non-growing clips that are shorter than 1 hour, the fully created transcript will be shown. Once the transcribe job has completed, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes and all transcript segments are automatically shown.
For growing (EWC) clips and or non-growing clips that are longer than 1 hour, the "Transcription in progress" message is replaced as soon as the first portion of the transcript is created.
You can monitor the job progress in the Process app. See "Monitoring Transcribe and Translate Jobs in the Process App" on page 379.
For transcription jobs submitted by you, you will receive a notification once the job is completed. Prerequisite: Sending notification has been enabled for the Process app in the User Settings > Notifications section. See "Using Process App Notifications" on page 384.
"Transcription failed" Check the error message of the failed job in the Process app. For transcription jobs submitted by you, you will receive a notification once a job failed (if sending notifications is enabled).
10 Using the Transcript Tab Reason/Action "No speech found" The audio track for which the transcript has been created does not contain spoken words.
"No speech found yet" (EWC clip or non-growing clip that is longer than 1 hour) The audio track for which the transcript is being created does not contain spoken words.
<empty grid> When the transcript is created for an EWC clip or a non-growing clip that is longer than 1 hour, an empty grid (means: only the Speaker and Transcript column headers) is shown for a short period of time before the first portions of the transcript are displayed.
The following messages are not displayed within in the Transcript tab but on the right side of the Fast bar and disappear after a short period of time.
Reason/Action "The transcript creation is completed" This message is only shown when the transcript creation for a growing (EWC) clip or a non-growing clip that is longer than 1 hour is finished. The message is not shown for non-growing clips that are shorter than 1 hour.
"The transcript creation failed" (EWC clip or non-growing clip that is longer than 1 hour) This message is shown when the transcribe job fails before completion and portions of the transcript are already displayed in the Transcript tab. Transcript data is kept and can be edited.
The following message is shown as a tool-tip for a warning icon on the Transcript tab toolbar.
Reason/Action "Transcript error: partial transcript (EWC clip or non-growing clip that is longer than 1 hour) If the only" transcript can only be partially displayed – transcript creation failed while portions of the transcript have already been shown on the Transcript tab – a warning icon is shown between the transcript selector and Find filter control. The tool-tip reads "Transcript error:
partial transcript only". Transcript data is kept and can be edited.
You can also create a translation for the partial transcript.
The following messages are not displayed within in the Transcript tab but are shown as a message when you use the Create Transcript App menu or context menu item in the Browse app or Search app and an error occurs. See "Creating a Transcript from the Browse and Search App" on page 358.
Reason/Action "No audio tracks found" The selected asset has no audio tracks, transcript creation is not possible.
"Failed to load asset info" The request to get asset information from the backend failed.
Accessing the Transcript of an Asset Accessing a transcript is a pretty basic operation, as described in the following procedure.
10 Using the Transcript Tab To access the transcript of an asset:
1. Use the Browse app, Search app, or other method to load a master clip or subclip into the Asset
Editor.
2. Click the Asset Editor’s Transcript tab.
If the Transcript tab is not shown, click the chevron on the right-side of the Tabs area to move the list of tab names and thus show the Transcript tab.
If the Transcript tab still is not shown, it might be hidden: Click the Tab menu button, hover your cursor over the Show/Hide Tabs option and select (enable) the Transcript check box. For more information, see "Asset Editor Tabs" on page 75.
Once the Transcript tab is shown in the Tabs area, click on Transcript.
The Transcript tab opens in Transcript mode. If there is a transcript available at least for one audio track, the Transcript tab shows the first transcript selected in the transcript selection control.
3. To access another transcript, select the transcript from the Transcript selection control.
Navigating within the Transcript When you play back a master clip or subclip in the Asset Editor's Media Viewer or position the playhead in the Asset Editor's timeline, text in the transcript will closely follow the spoken word in the clip.
Note the following when navigating the transcript:
10 Using the Transcript Tab If the Sync Playhead toggle button is selected (blue button, default), during playback in the Asset
- Editor's Media Viewer or when you position the playhead in the timeline, the corresponding word is
highlighted in green in the Transcript area.
Vice versa, when you select a section in the Transcript area, the playhead in the Asset Editor's Media Viewer is positioned to the corresponding timecode.
If the Sync Playhead toggle button is deselected (gray), during playback in the Asset Editor's Media
- Viewer, corresponding words are not highlighted in the Transcript area. Selecting a word in the
Transcript area does not move the playhead in the Asset Editor's Media Viewer.
- When you enable the Edit Mode button to edit the transcript, Sync Playhead is deactivated.
To navigate the transcript:
1. Open an asset in the Asset Editor.
2. Open the Transcript tab.
The Transcript tab opens in Transcript mode.
3. (optional) Click the Orientation menu in the Asset Editor toolbar and select the dual column view.
4. Select a transcript which you want to view from the transcript selection control.
5. To navigate with enabled synchronization:
a. (if inactive) Activate the Sync Playhead button.
b. Start playback or move the playhead in the timeline.
  - The currently spoken word is highlighted in green in the Transcript area, its segment is
highlighted in gray. While playback continues, playhead and highlighting update in tandem, the Transcript area scrolls down the list of transcript segments.
  - When the position is between two words, the left word in the Transcript tab is
highlighted.
  - When two words share the same timecode, both words are highlighted in green in the
Transcript tab.
  - When the position is between two segments (gap), the left segment is highlighted in the
Transcript tab.
c. Click on a segment in the Transcript tab.
The first word is highlighted in green and the playhead is moved to the corresponding timecode in the timeline.
10 Using the Transcript Tab d. Click on a word in the Transcript tab.
The word is highlighted in light green and the playhead is moved to the corresponding timecode in the timeline.
6. To navigate with disabled synchronization
a. Deactivate the Sync Playhead button so it turns gray.
b. Start playback or move the playhead in the timeline.
Neither words nor segments are highlighted in the Transcript area.
c. Click on a segment or word in the Transcript area.
Neither the video in the Asset Editor nor the playhead in the timeline is updated.
Searching in the Transcript If a transcript includes a large number of segments, use the Find field to search for words in the transcript segments.
Using the Find field navigation controls you can skip through the found words; if Sync Playhead is enabled, skipping through the found words also moves the playhead. See "Searching in the Transcript" above.
Note the following:
Search is only applied to the text in the Transcript column. To filter the transcript by speakers, use the
- Speaker filter. See "Filtering Speakers" on the next page.
The Find feature interacts with the Speaker filter:
  - If you already applied a Speaker filter, search is run in the Transcript column of the filtered
segments.
  - If you already used the Find field to search for words in the Transcript column, the search term,
word count and word highlighting are cleared when you apply a Speaker filter.
To search in the transcript:
1. Select the transcript which you want to view from the transcript selection control.
2. Click in the "Find" field and type the word you want for search for.
Since a "contains" search will be applied you do not need to type the entire word. Searching for multiple words is not supported.
3. Press Enter.
All words in the transcript that contain your search term are highlighted in blue. The transcript scrolls down to the segment with the first matching word, and selects the word.
10 Using the Transcript Tab
  - If Sync Playhead is enabled, the word is highlighted in green. In this case, also the playhead is
moved to the corresponding position in the timeline.
  - If Sync Playhead is disabled, the word in still highlighted in blue. In this case, the playhead is
not moved in the timeline.
The word count to the right side of the Find field shows the number of matching words in the form <selected word>/<total number of found word>.
4. Navigate through the matching words in the transcript by clicking the Previous or Next button.
For the first hit, the Previous button is disabled; when you reach the last hit, the Next button is disabled.
5. To remove the search term and word highlighting, click the X button in the Find field.
Filtering Speakers If a transcript includes a large number of segments, you can use the Speaker filtering control to display a subset of the list. Only the segments of the speaker(s) you select will be shown, all segments of other speakers will be hidden.
When you already used the Find field to search for words in the Transcript, the search term, word
- count and word highlighting are cleared when you apply a Speaker filter.
- Any selection in the Transcript area is cleared when you apply a Speaker filter.
To filter Speakers in the transcript:
1. Select the transcript which you want to view from the transcript selection control.
2. Click the gray Filter button in the Speaker column head.
3. In the filter tool that opens, select the check box of the speaker(s) you want to use.
You can use the Find a speaker field to search for speakers within the filter tool.
10 Using the Transcript Tab As soon as you select a speaker, the filter is applied. The Filter button turns blue to indicate an active filter.
4. Click outside the filter tool to close it.
Only the segments of the selected speaker(s) are shown in the Transcript area.
To turn off filtering:
- Click the Reload button.
- (If available) Select another transcript from the transcript selection control and then select the
original transcript again.
- Disable the filter manually:
1. Click the blue Filter button in the Speaker column head.
2. In the filter tool that opens, deselect all selected check boxes.
3. Click outside the filter tool.
All segments of all speakers are shown again in the Transcript area.
Copying Transcript Text to the Clipboard You can copy the text of transcript segments to the system clipboard and paste them into another application. Note that copied segments do not include the speaker name and time-stamp information.
The procedure of copying segment text is the same independently if Edit Mode is enabled or disabled.
To copy the text within a segment to the clipboard:
1. Highlight words by using the mouse pointer.
2. Do one of the following:
- Right-click the selection and in the context-menu that opens select Copy Text.
- Press Ctrl+C.
To copy the text of an individual segment to the clipboard:
- Right-click the segment and in the context-menu that opens select Copy Text.
- Click on the segment and press Ctrl+C.
10 Using the Transcript Tab To copy the text of several entire segments to the clipboard:
1. Select a segment, then press Shift+click to select a range or Ctrl+click to select individual segments.
2. Do one of the following:
- Right-click the selection and in the context-menu that opens select Copy Text.
- Press Ctrl+C.
To copy text across segments to the clipboard:
1. Click in a segment and move the pressed-down mouse pointer up or down to select text across
segments.
2. Do one of the following:
- Right-click the selection and in the context-menu that opens select Copy Text.
- Press Ctrl+C.
Exporting the Transcript The Transcript tab lets you export each transcript to your local workstation. The exported file contains each segment of each speaker including the time-stamp information.
To export a transcript:
1. Select the transcript which you want to export from the transcript selection control.
2. Click the Export Transcript button in the Transcript tab toolbar. If the button is not shown, click the
More Options button and select Export Transcript from the menu.
3. Select the file format from the menu. TXT, CSV and SRT are supported.
An <asset name>.txt, <asset name>.csv or <asset name>.srt file is saved to the download location specified in your web browser’s settings.
> **Note:** Depending on your browser settings, you might be prompted for a download location.
The following illustration shows an example of an exported transcript in TXT format.
10 Using the Transcript Tab The following illustration shows an example of an exported transcript in CSV format.
The following illustration shows an example of an exported transcript in SRT format. Note that timecodes are shown in Subtitles TimeCode format and row splitting in the *.srt file is different than in the Transcript tab.
Creating a Transcript Transcripts for assets can be created by automated workflows or by manual interaction in the Transcript tab. The following use cases are supported in the Transcript tab:
Creating the initial transcript for an entire asset
- Creating transcript for an additional audio track
- Triggering Re-transcribe for a track that has already a transcript
10 Using the Transcript Tab Creating transcripts is limited by specific user privileges. But it is also covered with a quota license that tracks and limits the number of hours of transcribed audio. Therefore, check your company policy before you create transcripts. Once the license quota has exceeded, creating transcripts will fail; in the Process app you will see "License quota exceeded: ent_mcs_count_stt" on each failed Media Analytics job.
For more information, see the following topics:
- "Creating the Initial Transcript for an Entire Asset" below
You can start transcript creation for a master clip but also from a subclip. Triggering transcript creation from a subclip will transcribe the whole master clip. If the master clip already has a transcript, a re-transcribe action is triggered for the master clip.
Additionally, you have the option to trigger transcript creation outside the Transcript tab. See the following topic:
- Creating the Initial Transcript for an Entire Asset
To create the initial transcript for an asset, proceed as follows.
You can start transcript creation for a master clip but also from a subclip. Triggering transcript creation from a subclip will transcribe the whole master clip.
To create the initial transcript:
1. Open an asset in the Transcript tab.
2. (if not selected) Click the Transcript button to switch to Transcript mode.
If the asset has no transcript, the "There is no transcript yet" message is shown.
Only for users of a group that has the entitlement Avid AI Transcribe RW Access assigned, additionally "You can generate a transcript for the whole asset" and a Transcribe Asset button are shown.
The transcript selection in the Transcript tab toolbar is empty.
3. Click the Transcribe Asset button.
The Create Transcript dialog box opens. For a master clip, the following is shown:
10 Using the Transcript Tab For a sublip, the following is shown:
4. (optional) Enter a name for the transcript to be created in the Transcript Name field.
If you do not enter a name, the audio track name is used as transcript name.
5. Select the track(s) for which a transcript is to be generated from the Audio Track list.
The Audio Track list shows the available audio tracks of the asset — but not more than 16.
> **Note:** If you select more than one audio track, the tracks will be mixed down into one track for
transcription and a transcript for the mixed down audio track is created.
6. Click the Transcribe button.
The dialog box is closed and the transcript creation job is triggered. The Transcript tab shows a "Transcription in progress" message.
10 Using the Transcript Tab If the transcript creation cannot be triggered, a "Failed to create transcript" error message is shown and the Create Transcript dialog box is not closed. Click Cancel to close the dialog box.
7. (optional) Open the Process app and monitor the transcript creation job. See "Monitoring Transcribe
and Translate Jobs in the Process App" on page 379.
8. Wait until the transcript is shown.
  - For non-growing clips that are shorter than 1 hour, the fully created transcript is shown. Once
the transcribe job has completed, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes and all transcript segments are automatically shown.
If the transcribe job fails before completion, the Transcription in progress message will be replaced by the Transcription failed message.
  - When a transcript is created for a growing (EWC) clip or a non-growing clip that is longer than
1 hour, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes, new portions of the transcript are automatically shown. As long as the transcript creation is not finished, the shown transcript segments are in read-only mode. When the transcript creation is finished, the Transcript tab auto-refreshes and enables editing of the transcript.
If the transcribe job fails before completion, a message "The transcript creation failed" is shown for a short period of time on the Fast bar, and a warning icon is displayed between the transcript selector and Find control on the toolbar. Its tool-tip reads "Transcription error:
partial transcript only". Already displayed portions of the transcript are kept and can be edited.
Creating Transcript for Selected Audio Tracks For an asset that already has a transcript, you can do the following:
10 Using the Transcript Tab Create a transcript for an additional audio track.
- Re-create the transcript for an audio track.
If a re-transcribe job for an audio track with existing transcript is triggered, all edits made to the transcript will be overwritten.
Triggering transcript creation from a subclip will transcribe the whole master clip. If the master clip already has a transcript, a re-transcribe action for the master clip is triggered.
The workflow for both use cases is quite the same, as described in the following procedure. Note that the Create New Transcription button is only enabled for users of a group that has the entitlement Avid AI Transcribe RW Access assigned.
To create the transcript for selected tracks:
1. Open an asset in the Transcript tab.
2. (if not selected) Click the Transcript button to switch to Transcript view.
The first available transcript is shown. The transcript selection in the Transcript tab toolbar shows all completed, failed or currently being created transcripts of the asset.
3. Click the Create New Transcript or Translation button in the Transcript tab toolbar and then select
Create New Transcript.
If the button is not shown, click the More Options button and select Create New Transcript or Translation > Create New Transcript from the menu.
The Create Transcript dialog box opens. For a subclip, the dialog box shows the warning that the whole master clip will be transcribed, not only the subclip.
4. (optional) Enter a name for the transcript to be created in the Transcript Name field.
If you do not enter a name, the audio track name is used as transcript name.
5. Select the track(s) for which a transcript is to be generated from the Audio Track list.
The Audio Track list shows the audio tracks of the asset.
> **Note:** If you select more than one audio track, the tracks will be mixed down into one track and a
transcript for the mixed down audio track is created. For example, if you select A1 and A2, a mixed down transcript for A1-A2 is created. If you want to create separate transcripts for A1 and A2, first create the transcript for A1, then for A2.
6. Click the Transcribe button.
The dialog box is closed and the transcript creation job is triggered.
If a selected audio track has already a completed transcript or a transcript currently being created, a Re-transcribe dialog box opens; for a track with failed transcript, the dialog box is not shown, re- transcription is started directly. The Re-transcribe dialog box shows one of the following messages:
10 Using the Transcript Tab
  - "The transcript for this channel(s) is already completed, would you like to re-transcribe?"
  - "The transcript for this channel(s) is in progress, would you like to re-transcribe?"
Do one of the following:
- Click Cancel to keep the already existing transcript/to continue with the already running
transcript creation.
- Click Re-transcribe to discard the already existing transcript/to stop the currently running
transcript creation, and initiate the re-creation of the transcript.
7. Select the transcript currently being created from the transcript selection.
The view does not automatically switch to another transcript.
Example: Transcript A1 was shown in the Transcript tab when you triggered transcript creation for track A2; the Transcript tab will not automatically switch to A2 but keeps on showing transcript A1.
You need to select A2 from the transcript selection.
8. (optional) Open the Process app and monitor the transcript creation job. See "Monitoring Transcribe
and Translate Jobs in the Process App" on page 379.
9. Wait until the transcript is shown.
  - For non-growing clips that are shorter than 1 hour, the fully created transcript is shown. Once
the transcribe job has completed, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes and all transcript segments are automatically shown.
If the transcribe job fails before completion, the Transcription in progress message will be replaced by the Transcription failed message.
  - When a transcript is created for a growing (EWC) clip or a non-growing clip that is longer than
1 hour, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes, new portions of the transcript are automatically shown. As long as the transcript creation is not finished, the shown transcript segments are in read-only mode. When the transcript creation is finished, the Transcript tab auto-refreshes and enables editing of the transcript.
If the transcribe job fails before completion, a message "The transcript creation failed" is shown for a short period of time on the Fast bar, and a warning icon is displayed between the transcript selector and Find control on the toolbar. Its tool-tip reads "Transcription error:
partial transcript only". Already displayed portions of the transcript are kept and can be edited.
Creating a Transcript from the Browse and Search App In addition to the Transcript tab, MediaCentral Cloud UX provides the option to trigger transcript creation in the Browse and Search app. In these cases, you do not need to open the asset in the Transcript tab to create the transcript. The Create Transcript option is only shown in the Browse and Search apps in the following cases:
10 Using the Transcript Tab If a single master clip or subclip of an Avid Production Management/local Production Management
- module is selected.
Multi-selection is not supported.
Triggering transcript creation from a subclip will transcribe the whole master clip. If the master clip already has a transcript, a re-transcribe action for the master clip is triggered.
- For users of a group that has the entitlement Avid AI Transcribe RW Access assigned.
- When the feature pack STT is installed and the required license is activated.
To create a transcript for a master clip or subclip open in the Browse or Search app:
1. Do one of the following:
- In the Browse app, open a folder of an Avid Production Management/local Production
Management module that contains master clips or subclips or both.
- In the Search app, conduct a search for a master clip or subclip from an Avid Production
Management/local Production Management module.
2. Do one of the following:
- Select a master clip or subclip, then click the App Menu and select the Create Transcript menu
option.
- Right-click a master clip or subclip and select Create Transcript from the context menu.
The context menu for a master clip in the Browse app (left) and Search app (right) The Create Transcript dialog box opens. For a subclip, the dialog box shows the warning that the whole master clip will be transcribed, not only the subclip.
3. (optional) Enter a name for the transcript to be created in the Transcript Name field.
If you do not enter a name, the audio track name is used as transcript name.
4. Select the track(s) for which a transcript is to be generated from the Audio Track list.
The Audio Track list shows the audio tracks of the asset.
> **Note:** If you select more than one audio track, the tracks will be mixed down into one track and a
transcript for the mixed down audio track is created. For example, if you select A1 and A2, a mixed down transcript for A1-A2 is created. If you want to create separate transcripts for A1 and A2, first create the transcript for A1, then for A2.
5. Click the Transcribe button.
The dialog box is closed and the transcript creation job is triggered.
10 Using the Transcript Tab If a selected audio track has already a completed transcript or a transcript currently being created, a Re-transcribe dialog box opens; for a track with failed transcript, the dialog box is not shown, re- transcription is started directly. The Re-transcribe dialog box shows one of the following messages:
  - "The transcript for this channel(s) is already completed, would you like to re-transcribe?"
  - "The transcript for this channel(s) is in progress, would you like to re-transcribe?"
Do one of the following:
- Click Cancel to keep the already existing transcript/to continue with the already running
transcript creation.
- Click Re-transcribe to discard the already existing transcript/to stop the currently running
transcript creation, and initiate the re-creation of the transcript.
All edits previously made to the existing transcript are overwritten.
6. (optional) Open the Process app and monitor the transcript creation job. See "Monitoring Transcribe
and Translate Jobs in the Process App" on page 379.
7. (optional) Open the Transcript tab and wait until the transcript is shown.
  - For non-growing clips that are shorter than 1 hour, the fully created transcript is shown. Once
the transcribe job has completed, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes and all transcript segments are automatically shown.
If the transcribe job fails before completion, the Transcription in progress message will be replaced by the Transcription failed message.
  - When a transcript is created for a growing (EWC) clip or a non-growing clip that is longer than
1 hour, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes, new portions of the transcript are automatically shown. As long as the transcript creation is not finished, the shown transcript segments are in read-only mode. When the transcript creation is finished, the Transcript tab auto-refreshes and enables editing of the transcript.
If the transcribe job fails before completion, a message "The transcript creation failed" is shown for a short period of time on the Fast bar, and a warning icon is displayed between the transcript selector and Find control on the toolbar. Its tool-tip reads "Transcription error:
partial transcript only". Already displayed portions of the transcript are kept and can be edited.
Editing a Transcript Once a transcript is completely created, you can edit it. The transcript portions already shown for EWC clips or very long clips stay in read-only mode until the entire transcribe operation successfully finishes.
10 Using the Transcript Tab Avid Transcribe uses an Artificial Intelligence (AI) / Machine Learning (ML) model to produce transcripts and speaker labels from assets that include non-musical, spoken word. In terms of a newsroom workflow, this might be identified as a VO or "talking head". As with all AI/ML models, results might be incorrect as the model is predicting the output from the provided input. Therefore, the Transcript tab provides options to correct any of these errors.
For more information on these editing options, see the following topics:
- "Renaming Transcripts" below
- "Renaming Speakers" on the next page
You can edit speakers only for a transcript but not for a translation: The Add speaker, Rename speaker, Delete speaker and Assign segment to another speaker features are disabled when Translation mode is enabled in the Transcript tab.
When you open a subclip in the Transcript tab and edit the transcript, you change the transcript of the master clip.
All edits you make to a transcript will be overwritten if a re-transcribe job for the same audio track is triggered.
Renaming Transcripts The transcript name is shown in the transcript selection list. A transcript name can be created as follows:
Set in the Transcript Name field in the Create Transcript dialog box.
- Set in the Transcript Name field in a Media Analytics action in Rules Editor or Schedule Editor.
- If the name was not provided, the transcript is named according to the audio track(s) for which it was
- created.
You can rename a transcript at any time, as described in the following procedure. Renaming a transcript is supported in both modes of the Transcript tab (Transcript and Translation).
To rename a transcript:
1. Open the transcript selection.
2. Hover the cursor over the transcript you want to rename.
An edit button (pencil) is shown.
3. Click the edit button.
10 Using the Transcript Tab The Rename Transcript dialog box is shown.
4. Type the new transcript name in the text field.
The Save button is enabled.
5. Click Save.
The Rename Transcript dialog box is closed. The name change is directly applied.
In the following example, A1 was renamed to English.
For a renamed transcript, the track name is additionally shown in brackets. If the name cannot be shown completely in the transcript selection, you can see the full name and track in a tool-tip.
Renaming Speakers When a transcript is created, each speaker is identified by an ID and a name "Speaker <number>". You can edit each speaker name to make it a person's name. In the following example, Speaker 2 will be renamed to John.
To rename a speaker:
1. Click the Edit Mode button so it turns blue.
2. Right-click a speaker name in the Speaker column.
3. In the context menu that appears, select Rename.
10 Using the Transcript Tab The speaker name field becomes a editable field. The speaker name is highlighted.
4. Type the new speaker name.
5. Click outside the field.
All instances of the speaker name in the transcript are updated. This applies also to the speaker name in the Change Speaker control.
If you deleted the entire speaker name while editing, the previous name is restored when you click outside the field.
Creating New Speakers When you inspect a transcript and identify a speaker that is not included in the speakers list, you can add the missing speaker name.
To create a new speaker:
1. Click the Edit Mode button so it turns blue.
2. Do one of the following:
- Select word(s) in a Transcript segment and right-click the selection.
- Right-click a segment or a speaker name.
- Select a segment, then press Shift+click to select a range or Ctrl+click to select individual
segments and right-click the selection.
- Click in a segment and move the pressed-down mouse pointer up or down to select text across
segments, then right-click the selection.
3. In the context menu that appears, select Add New Speaker.
The Add New Speaker dialog box opens.
10 Using the Transcript Tab
4. Type the name of the speaker to be created in the Speaker field.
5. Click the Add button.
If a speaker with the same name already exists in the transcript, a corresponding message opens.
Click outside the message, provide a unique name in the Add New Speaker dialog box and click the Add button again.
The Add New Speaker dialog box is closed. Depending on the source on which the Add New Speaker action was started (selection of words, text selected across segments, individual segment or several segments), the following happens:
  - A new segment with the selected word(s) is created for the new speaker.
  - The selected segment is assigned to the new speaker.
  - The selected segments are assigned to the new speaker.
  - The text selection is assigned to the new speaker.
The new name is shown in the Speakers column.
Deleting Speakers If you find out that the list of speakers contains speakers that are not used in the transcript any longer, you can correct this. Note that you can only delete speakers that are not assigned to any segment in the transcript.
To delete a speaker:
1. Click the Edit Mode button so it turns blue.
2. Right-click a segment or speaker name.
3. In the context menu that appears, select Change Speaker, then scroll down the speaker names.
Only for speakers that are not used in the transcript a Delete button (trash bin icon) is shown to the right side of the name.
10 Using the Transcript Tab
4. Click the Delete button.
The speaker is removed from the Change Speaker control. The control remains open to let you apply additional changes.
Assigning Transcript Segment to Another Speaker If you find out that a portion of the transcript is not assigned to the right speaker, you can correct this. You have the following options:
Assign portions of a segment to another speaker.
- Assign one or several segments to another speaker.
- To assign portions of a segment to another speaker:
1. Click the Edit Mode button so it turns blue.
2. Scroll to the segment that contains portions to be assigned to another speaker.
3. Select the word(s) to be re-assigned:
a. Click on a word to be re-assigned.
The selected word is highlighted.
b. To create a selection of words, highlight the words by using the mouse cursor.
4. Right-click the selection.
5. In the context menu that appears, select Change Speaker > <speaker name>.
The selected words are assigned to the new speaker.
Depending on the time stamp of the words, the following happens:
  - The words are added to a previous segment of the speaker.
  - The words are added to a following segment of the speaker.
  - A new segment with the words is created for the speaker.
10 Using the Transcript Tab To assign segments or text across segments to another speaker:
1. Click the Edit Mode button so it turns blue.
2. Do one of the following:
- Right-click a segment that you want to assign to another speaker.
- Select a segment, then press Shift+click to select a range or Ctrl+click to select individual
segments and right-click the selection.
- Click in a segment and move the pressed-down mouse pointer up or down to select text across
segments, then right-click the selection.
3. In the context menu that appears, select Change Speaker > <speaker name>.
The segment, selection or segments are assigned to the new speaker; the name is updated in the Speakers column.
Editing the Transcript Text You can edit the transcript text, for example, to correct wrong names.
Note the following limitations for text editing:
You can edit only one word at a time.
- You cannot delete a word.
- You cannot add and remove blank space between characters or words.
- As a side effect, you cannot modify a word containing an apostrophe when the change would
introduce a blank space. For example, changing "it's" to "it is" would be denied.
When you enter the Edit Mode while the asset is played back in the Media Viewer, playback will be stopped; the Synchronize Playhead feature is ignored even if the toggle is enabled.
To correct words in the transcript:
1. Click the Edit Mode button so it turns blue.
2. Scroll to the segment that contains text you want to modify.
3. Double-click the word you want to modify.
The word is highlighted and underlined and enabled for editing.
4. Type the new text.
5. Press Enter or click outside the field.
10 Using the Transcript Tab Your change is applied.
If your changes cannot be applied – because, for example, you added a blank space or deleted the word – the following message is shown on the right side of the Fast Bar. You can click the Try again link to repeat the update request. The message disappears after a few seconds.
Using Translations When you open an asset in the Transcript tab, you see the transcript selected in the transcript selection control. Each asset can have multiple transcripts, and each transcript can have multiple translations. For each transcript, you can create, view and edit translations.
When you open a subclip in the Transcript tab, the subclip-related transcript and translation parts of the underlying master clip are shown.
See the following topics:
"Accessing the Translation of a Transcript" below
- Accessing the Translation of a Transcript
Accessing a translation of a transcript is a pretty basic operation, as described in the following procedure.
To access the translation of a transcript:
1. Load a master clip or subclip into the Asset Editor and click the Asset Editor’s Transcript tab.
The Transcript tab is opened in Transcript view.
If there is a transcript available at least for one audio track, the Transcript tab shows the first transcript selected in the transcript selection control.
2. Click the Translation button to switch to Translation mode.
3. Select a transcript from the Transcript selection control.
10 Using the Transcript Tab If there is at least one translation available for the transcript, the tab shows the first translation for the transcript.
4. Select a language from the Translation selection control.
The translation is shown in the Transcript tab.
In some cases, you might see the following instead of the translation.
Reason/Action Translation view toggle button is There is no transcript for the asset. Before you can create or access a disabled translation, the transcript must have been created. Possible reasons:
- No transcript has been created for the asset at all.
- The transcript creation job is still running.
Create New Translation menu item is
- The transcript creation failed.
disabled
- The transcript creation finished with a No speech found
message.
See "Creating the Initial Transcript for an Entire Asset" on page 354.
"There are no translations yet" The selected transcript has no translation. Select another transcript of the asset or consider triggering the translation creation.
The translation selection is dimmed and does not show a language code See "Creating the Initial Translation for a Transcript" on the next page "Translation in progress" Wait until the translation job is completed.
Once the translation job has completed, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes and all translated segments are automatically shown.
You can monitor the job progress in the Process app. See "Monitoring Transcribe and Translate Jobs in the Process App" on page 379.
For translation jobs submitted by you, you will receive a notification once the job is completed. Prerequisite: Sending notification has been 10 Using the Transcript Tab Reason/Action enabled for the Process app in the User Settings > Notifications section. See "Using Process App Notifications" on page 384.
"Translation failed" Check the error message of the failed job in the Process app. For translation jobs submitted by you, you will receive a notification once a job failed (if sending notifications is enabled).
"We encountered a problem and the The translation could not be loaded from the server. Contact your translation couldn’t be loaded." administrator.
Creating the Initial Translation for a Transcript To create the initial translation for a transcript, proceed as follows.
You can start translation creation for a master clip but also from a subclip. Triggering translation creation from a subclip will translate the whole master clip.
To create the initial translation:
1. Open an asset in the Transcript tab.
2. Select the transcript from the Transcript selection control.
3. Click the Translation button to switch to Translation mode.
If the transcript has no translation, the "There are no translations yet" message is shown.
Only for users of a group that has the entitlement Avid AI Translate RW Access assigned, additionally "You can generate a translation for the selected transcription" and a Translate button are shown.
The translation selection in the Transcript tab toolbar is empty.
4. Click the Translate button.
The Translate dialog box opens. For a master clip, the following is shown:
10 Using the Transcript Tab For a sublip, the following is shown:
5. Do not change the transcript that is already selected in the Transcript list.
6. Select the language of the translation from the Target Language list.
The list shows the localized labels of all supported languages. If your administrator has defined a default translation language in the Avid AI admin app, the language is preselected in the Target Language list; if no default translation language has been defined, the top-most language in the list is preselected.
7. Click the Translate button.
The dialog box is closed and the translation creation job is triggered. The Transcript tab shows a "Translation in progress" message.
10 Using the Transcript Tab If the translation creation cannot be triggered, a "Failed to create translation" error message is shown and the Translate dialog box is not closed. Click Cancel to close the dialog box.
8. (optional) Open the Process app and monitor the translation creation job. See "Monitoring Transcribe
and Translate Jobs in the Process App" on page 379.
9. Wait until the translation is shown.
  - Once the translate job has completed, the Translate view display auto-refreshes and all
translated segments are automatically shown.
  - If the translate job fails before completion, the Translation in progress message will be
replaced by the Translation failed message.
Creating an Additional Translation for a Transcript For a transcript that already has a translation, you can do the following:
Create an additional translation.
- Re-create the translation.
If a re-translate job for a transcript with existing translation is triggered, all edits made to the translation will be overwritten.
Triggering translation creation from a subclip will translate the whole master clip. If the master clip already has a translation, a re-translate action for the master clip is triggered.
The workflow for both use cases is quite the same, as described in the following procedure. Note that the Create New Translation button is only enabled for users of a group that has the entitlement Avid AI Translate RW Access assigned.
To create an additional translation:
1. Open an asset in the Transcript tab.
2. Select the transcript from the Transcript selection control.
3. Click the Translation button to switch to Translation view.
If there is at least one translation available for the transcript, the tab shows the first translation for the transcript.
4. Click the Create New Transcript or Translation button in the Transcript tab toolbar and then select
Create New Translation.
If the button is not shown, click the More Options button and select Create New Transcript or Translation > Create New Translation from the menu.
The Translate dialog box opens. For a subclip, the dialog box shows the warning that the whole master clip will be translated, not only the subclip.
5. (optional) Select the transcript for which a translation is to be generated from the Transcript list.
10 Using the Transcript Tab The Transcript list shows the available transcripts of the asset.
6. Select the language of the translation from the Target Language list.
7. Click the Translate button.
The dialog box is closed and the translation creation job is triggered.
If a selected transcript has already a completed translation or a translation currently being created, a Re-translate dialog box opens; for a transcript with failed translation, the dialog box is not shown, re-translation is started directly. The Re-translate dialog box shows one of the following messages:
  - "The translation you selected for this transcription is already completed, would you like to re-
translate?"
  - "The translation you selected for this transcription is in progress, would you like to re-
translate?" Do one of the following:
- Click Cancel to keep the already existing translation/to continue with the already running
translation creation.
- Click Re-translate to discard the already existing translation/to stop the currently running
translation creation, and initiate the re-creation of the translation.
8. Select the translation currently being created from the translation selection.
The view does not automatically switch to another translation.
Example: The translation French was shown in the Translations view when you triggered translation creation for the language German; the Translations view will not automatically switch to German but keeps on showing French. You need to select German from the translation selection.
9. (optional) Open the Process app and monitor the translation creation job. See "Monitoring Transcribe
and Translate Jobs in the Process App" on page 379.
10. Wait until the translation is shown.
  - Once the translate job has completed, the Translate view display auto-refreshes and all
translated segments are automatically shown.
  - If the translate job fails before completion, the Translation in progress message will be
replaced by the Translation failed message.
Navigating within the Translation When you play back a master clip or subclip in the Asset Editor's Media Viewer or position the playhead in the Asset Editor's timeline, segments in the translation will follow the spoken word in the clip.
Note the following when navigating the translation:
10 Using the Transcript Tab If the Sync Playhead toggle button is selected (blue button, default), during playback in the Asset
- Editor's Media Viewer or when you position the playhead in the timeline, the segment that contains
the corresponding word is highlighted in green in the Translation area.
Vice versa, when you select a segment in the Translation area, the playhead in the Asset Editor's Media Viewer is positioned to the start timecode of the segment.
If the Sync Playhead toggle button is deselected (gray), during playback in the Asset Editor's Media
- Viewer, corresponding segments are not highlighted in the Translation area. Selecting a segment in
the Translation area does not move the playhead in the Asset Editor's Media Viewer.
- When you enable the Edit Mode button to edit the translation, Sync Playhead is deactivated.
To navigate the translation:
1. Open an asset in the Asset Editor.
2. Open the Transcript tab.
The Transcript tab opens in Transcript mode.
3. Click the Translation button to switch to Translation mode.
4. (optional) Click the Orientation menu in the Asset Editor toolbar and select the dual column view.
5. Select a transcript from the transcript selection control.
6. Select the translation which you want to view from the translation selection control.
7. To navigate with enabled synchronization:
a. (if inactive) Activate the Sync Playhead button.
b. Start playback or move the playhead in the timeline.
  - The segment that contains the currently spoken word is highlighted in green in the
Translation area. While playback continues, playhead and highlighting update in tandem, the Translation area scrolls down the list of translation segments.
  - When the position is between two segments (gap), the left segment is highlighted in the
Transcript tab.
c. Click on a segment in the Transcript tab.
10 Using the Transcript Tab The entire segment is highlighted in green and the playhead is moved to the start timecode of the segment in the timeline.
8. To navigate with disabled synchronization
a. Deactivate the Sync Playhead button so it turns gray.
b. Start playback or move the playhead in the timeline.
No segments are highlighted in the Translation area.
c. Click on a segment in the Translation area.
Neither the video in the Asset Editor nor the playhead in the timeline is updated.
Searching in the Translation If a translation includes a large number of segments, use the Find field to search for words in the translation segments.
For a translation, the results count shows you the number of segments in which the word is found, it does not show the number of found words.
Using the Find field navigation controls you can skip through the found segments; if Sync Playhead is enabled, skipping through the found segments also moves the playhead. See "Navigating within the Translation" on page 372.
Note the following:
- Search is only applied to the text in the Transcript column. To filter the translation by speakers, use
the Speaker filter. The procedure on how to filter speakers is the same in Transcript and Translation mode. See "Filtering Speakers" on page 350.
The Find feature interacts with the Speaker filter:
  - If you already applied a Speaker filter, search is run in the Transcript column of the filtered
segments.
  - If you already used the Find field to search for words in the Transcript column, the search term,
segment count and segment highlighting are cleared when you apply a Speaker filter.
To search in the translation:
1. Select the translation which you want to view from the translation selection control.
2. Click in the "Find" field and type the word you want for search for.
Since a "contains" search will be applied you do not need to type the entire word. Searching for multiple words is not supported.
3. Press Enter.
10 Using the Transcript Tab All segments in the translation that contain your search term are highlighted in blue. The translation scrolls down to the first matching segment, and selects it.
  - If Sync Playhead is enabled, the selected segment is highlighted in green. In this case, also the
playhead is moved to the corresponding position in the timeline.
  - If Sync Playhead is disabled, the segment in still highlighted in blue. In this case, the playhead
is not moved in the timeline.
The segment count to the right side of the Find field shows the number of matching segments in the form <selected segment>/<total number of found segments>.
4. Navigate through the matching segments in the translation by clicking the Previous or Next button.
For the first hit, the Previous button is disabled; when you reach the last hit, the Next button is disabled.
5. To remove the search term and segment highlighting, click the X button in the Find field.
Copying Translation Text to the Clipboard You can copy the text of translation segments to the system clipboard and paste them into another application. Note that copied segments do not include the speaker name and time-stamp information.
Only for copying text within a segment Edit Mode needs to be enabled.
To copy the text within a segment to the clipboard:
1. Click the Edit Mode button so it turns blue.
2. Double-click the segment that contains the text you want to copy.
The cursor is positioned at the end of the segment; if required, the Translation area display scrolls to show the cursor position.
3. Highlight words by using the mouse pointer.
10 Using the Transcript Tab
4. Do one of the following:
- Right-click the selection and in the context-menu that opens select Copy Text.
- Press Ctrl+C.
To copy the text of an individual segment to the clipboard:
- Right-click the segment and in the context-menu that opens select Copy Text.
- Click on the segment and press Ctrl+C.
To copy the text of several entire segments to the clipboard:
1. Select a segment, then press Shift+click to select a range or Ctrl+click to select individual segments.
2. Do one of the following:
- Right-click the selection and in the context-menu that opens select Copy Text.
- Press Ctrl+C.
Editing the Translation Text Once a translation is completely created, you can edit its text.
When you open a subclip in the Transcript tab and edit the translation, you change the translation of the master clip.
All edits you make to a translation will be overwritten if a re-translate job for the same translation is triggered.
You cannot edit speakers for a translation: The Add speaker, Rename speaker, Delete speaker and Assign segment to another speaker features are disabled when Translation mode is enabled in the Transcript tab.
However, you can filter the segment display by speakers. The procedure on how to filter speakers is the same in Transcript and Translation mode. See "Filtering Speakers" on page 350.
Note the following limitations for text editing:
- You can edit only one word at a time.
- You cannot delete a word.
- You cannot add and remove blank space between characters or words.
As a side effect, you cannot modify a word containing an apostrophe when the change would introduce a blank space. For example, changing "it's" to "it is" would be denied.
When you enter the Edit Mode while the asset is played back in the Media Viewer, playback will be stopped; the Synchronize Playhead feature is ignored even if the toggle is enabled.
To correct words in the translation:
1. Click the Edit Mode button so it turns blue.
2. Scroll to the segment that contains text you want to modify.
3. Double-click the segment that contains the word you want to modify.
The cursor is positioned at the end of the segment; if required, the Translation area display scrolls to show the cursor position.
4. Double-click the word you want to modify.
The word is highlighted.
10 Using the Transcript Tab
5. Type the new text.
6. Press Enter or click outside the field.
Your change is applied.
If your changes cannot be applied – because, for example, you added a blank space or deleted the word – the following message is shown on the right side of the Fast Bar. You can click the Try again link to repeat the update request. The message disappears after a few seconds.
Exporting the Translation The Transcript tab lets you export each translation of a transcript to your local workstation. The exported file contains each segment of each speaker including the time-stamp information.
To export a translation:
1. Select the translation which you want to export from the translation selection control.
2. Click the Export Translation button in the Transcript tab toolbar. If the button is not shown, click the
More Options button and select Export Translation from the menu.
3. Select the file format from the menu. TXT, CSV and SRT are supported.
An <asset name>.txt, <asset name>.csv or <asset name>.srt file is saved to the download location specified in your web browser’s settings.
> **Note:** Depending on your browser settings, you might be prompted for a download location.
Monitoring Transcript and Translation Creation See the following topics for more information:
- "Options within the Transcript Tab" on the next page
- "Using the Search App's Transcript Status Information" on the next page
10 Using the Transcript Tab Options within the Transcript Tab Transcript Creation How information is updated in the Transcript tab depends on if the transcript is being created for a growing (EWC) or for a non-growing clip. In both cases, the Transcript tab auto-updates, clicking the Reload button is not required.
How information is updated for a non-growing clip shorter than 1 hour:
For non-growing clips that are shorter than 1 hour, the fully created transcript is shown. Once the transcribe job has completed, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes and all transcript segments are automatically shown.
If the transcribe job fails before completion, the Transcription in progress message will be replaced by the Transcription failed message.
How information is updated for a growing clip or non-growing clip longer than 1 hour:
When a transcript is created for a growing (EWC) clip or a non-growing clip that is longer than 1 hour, the Transcript tab display auto-refreshes, new portions of the transcript are automatically shown. As long as the transcript creation is not finished, the shown transcript segments are in read-only mode. When the transcript creation is finished, the Transcript tab auto-refreshes and enables editing of the transcript.
If the transcribe job fails before completion, a message "The transcript creation failed" is shown for a short period of time on the Fast bar, and a warning icon is displayed between the transcript selector and Find control on the toolbar. Its tool-tip reads "Transcription error: partial transcript only". Already displayed portions of the transcript are kept and can be edited.
Translation Creation How information is updated:
Once the translate job has completed, the Translate view display auto-refreshes and all translated segments are automatically shown.
If the translate job fails before completion, the Translation in progress message will be replaced by the Translation failed message.
To get more information on the status of your transcribe or translate job, monitor the job progress in the Process app. See "Monitoring Transcribe and Translate Jobs in the Process App" on the next page.
Using the Search App's Transcript Status Information In addition to the Transcript tab and Process app, you can also get information about the transcript status of a master clip or subclip within the Search app. In the case of a subclip, the Transcript Status column reports the status of the parent master clip.
To use the Transcript Status information in the Search app:
1. Open the Search app.
2. Click the Display options drop-down menu.
3. Select the Transcript Status check box.
4. Click an area outside of the menu to apply your change.
The Transcript Status column is shown in the Results area on the far right.
You might reorder the column by dragging the column header to the left.
10 Using the Transcript Tab
5. Search for master clips or subclips or both of an Avid Production Management/local Production
Management module.
6. Inspect the information in the Transcript Status column.
Icon Transcript Status <none> The asset has not been transcribed (or attempted to be transcribed).
Pending: The transcribe job is in queue, waiting to be processed.
Running: The transcript creation is in progress.
Paused: The job was paused while it was processing.
Completed: The transcript job completed successfully.
Failed: The transcribe job failed to complete successfully.
Cancelled: The transcribe job was started and actively canceled.
Monitoring Transcribe and Translate Jobs in the Process App MediaCentral Cloud UX offers process monitoring features in the Process app. You can monitor the jobs you initiated when triggering transcript or translation creation and jobs of involved components. When signed into MediaCentral Cloud UX as a standard user, you are allowed to see only the jobs that you submitted. If an administrator enables the Master of Jobs entitlement for a user group, the users within that group can see the list of jobs submitted by all users.
By default, jobs of all types currently being used are shown as a list in the Process app that is sorted by the Name column in alphabetical order. You can modify the Process List to show only Transcribe and Translate jobs by using the following controls:
Control Action Groups filter (sidebar) The following Groups filters can be used for monitoring Transcribe jobs:
- STT@Avid DP ML
- Audio Extract
- Media Transcript
- Distributed Processing Job
- Distributed Processing Task
The following Groups filter can be used for monitoring Translate jobs:
- TranslateTranscript@Avid AI
System filter (sidebar) The following System filters can be used for monitoring Transcribe jobs:
Media Analytics
- Unknown (displays Distributed Processing Job and Distributed
- Processing Task)
- XForm (displays jobs of groups Media Transcript and Audio
Extract) 10 Using the Transcript Tab Control Action
> **Note:** The filters Unknown and XForm will also show jobs that are not
related to Transcribe.
The following System filter can be used for monitoring Translate jobs:
- Media Analytics
Show Hierarchy toggle (toolbar) To visualize the hierarchy of the jobs involved in the transcript or translation creation, select the Show Hierarchy toggle button in the toolbar.
All jobs involved in the process chain for one transcript or translation are shown as sub-jobs of a top-level Media Analytics job. Each top- level Media Analytics job shows the name of the asset for which the transcript or translation is currently being created and a prefix. For Transcribe jobs, the prefix is:
Transcribe: Shown for Transcribe jobs triggered from the
- Transcript tab or triggered by the default Media Analytics
Transcribe rule.
- Avid DP ML Transcribe: Shown for Transcribe jobs triggered by
custom Media Analytics rules.
For Translate jobs, the prefix is:
Translate: Shown for Translate jobs triggered from the
- Transcript tab.
- Avid AI Translation: Shown for Translate jobs triggered by
Media Analytics rules.
All filters are cumulative. The best approach to display Transcribe and Translate jobs in the Process app is to use the Groups filter and Show Hierarchy toggle, as described in the following procedure.
> **Note:** As a standard user, you only see the “Group” STT@Avid DP ML and “System” Media Analytics in the
Process app for the transcribe jobs you triggered in the Transcript tab. Only users of a group with Master of Jobs entitlement can see the all included groups and jobs.
To monitor Transcribe and Translate jobs in the Process app:
1. Click the Process button on the Fast Bar.
2. Open the Filters sidebar.
3. To show only transcribe jobs using "Groups":
a. Expand the Groups list in the Filters sidebar.
b. Click the "show all" button.
c. In the break-out window that opens. select the process groups you want to display and then click the Apply button.
  - For Transcribe: Audio Extract, Distributed Processing Job, Distributed Processing Task,
Media Transcript, STT@Avid DP ML
  - For Translate: TranslateTranscript@Avid AI and Translate
The Process app shows you only the corresponding jobs. Example for transcribe jobs:
10 Using the Transcript Tab Example for translate jobs:
4. To define the grouping behavior:
a. Click the Display Group button so it turns blue.
Jobs are grouped by the group property in the Process List.
Example for transcribe groups:
10 Using the Transcript Tab Example for translate groups:
b. Click the Expand/Collapse toggle button of each job group to show its jobs.
5. To visualize the job hierarchy for Transcribe:
a. Click the Show Hierarchy button in the Process app toolbar so it turns blue.
Sub-jobs are arranged below their parent job in the Process List.
Since the Groups filter is still applied and Transcribe jobs are parent jobs, the names of the other involved Groups are no longer shown.
a. Click the Expand button to the left side of the STT@Avid DP ML group header to show all top- level Transcribe jobs.
Each top-level Transcribe job shows the name of the asset for which the transcript is currently being created and a prefix (such as Transcribe or Avid DP ML Transcribe) in its name.
b. Click the Expand / Collapse toggle button in front of a top-level Transcribe job to show or hide its sub-jobs.
On the second hierarchy level, a Media Analytics<number> job for each partition is shown.
  - For EWC clips, the partition size is 3 minutes.
  - For non-growing clips, the partition size is 1 hour.
10 Using the Transcript Tab Below each Media Analytics partition job, the involved Audio Extract, Media Transform, Distributed Processing Job and Distributed Processing Task jobs are shown.
c. Click the Expand / Collapse toggle button in front of a parent job to show or hide its sub-jobs.
6. To visualize the job hierarchy for Translate:
a. Click the Show Hierarchy button in the Process app toolbar so it turns blue.
Sub-jobs are arranged below their parent job in the Process List.
Since the Groups filter is still applied and Translate jobs are parent jobs, the names of the other involved Groups are no longer shown.
a. Click the Expand button to the left side of the TranslateTranscript group header to show all top-level Translate jobs.
Each top-level Translate job shows the name of the asset for which the transcript translation is currently being created and a prefix (such as Translate or Avid AI Translation) in its name.
b. Click the Expand / Collapse toggle button in front of a top-level Translate job to show or hide its sub-jobs.
On the second hierarchy level, a Media Analytics<number> job for each partition is shown.
10 Using the Transcript Tab Below each Media Analytics partition job, the involved Media Analytics processor jobs are shown.
c. Click the Expand / Collapse toggle button in front of a parent job to show or hide its sub-jobs.
7. (optional) Apply additional filter criteria.
a. (Filters sidebar) To filter the jobs by creation time ranges, expand the Creation Time list and select one of the time ranges.
b. (Filters sidebar and Toolbar) To show only jobs of a specific life cycle, expand the Status list and select the Life cycle status you want to show or click the corresponding Status button in the toolbar. Multi-selection is supported.
Using Process App Notifications The Process app supports sending messages for your jobs that reached the final state (Canceled, Completed, Failed) to the Notifications app. This allows you to stay informed about your jobs without having the Process app open. To receive notifications about your completed jobs from the Process app, the notifications need to be enabled in the User Settings > Notifications section.
> **Note:** Enabling the Process app notifications will send notifications for all of your jobs in final state not only
notifications for Transcribe and Translate jobs.
To customize your notification options:
1. Click the User Profile button on the right side of the Fast Bar and select User Settings.
The User Settings dialog box opens.
2. In the navigation panel on the left, select the Notifications group.
3. In the Activity Notifications section, go to the Process app group and do one of the following:
- Click the toggle in the app’s header so it turns blue.
- Click the toggle of the Job Completed feature so it turns blue.
10 Using the Transcript Tab
4. Click the Save button.
Next time you trigger a transcribe job, the Process app sends a notification once the job has reached a final state, as shown in the following example.
5. (optional) Click on a notification.
The Process app opens. Note that the corresponding process will not be highlighted in the Process app.
(Administrators) Checking the License Quota Using Avid Transcribe features is covered with a quota license:
- Avid Ada Transcribe tracks and limits the number of hours of transcribed audio.
In the License app, for Transcribe the quantity equals the total number of minutes that can be transcribed within a cycle.
> **Note:** Depending on the time of purchase, the license might show the original name "MediaCentral |
STT Base Package" instead of "Avid Ada Transcribe".
10 Using the Transcript Tab Each cycle starts at the point of the license activation and ends after one year. The counter resets to zero when the cycle ends. For more information, see "Reviewing the Licensing Results Panel" in the Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX Installation Guide.
If you transcribe the maximum quota defined by the license within a cycle, the system stops processing any new media. In the Process app, a "License quota exceeded: ent_mcs_count_stt" will be shown on each failed Media Analytics job.
You can contact Avid Sales to purchase additional minutes as needed.
As an administrator, you can monitor the current quota of the licenses in the MediaCentral Cloud UX License app.
To check the license quota:
1. Sign-in to the MediaCentral Cloud UX Administrators apps.
2. Select the License app from the Fast Bar.
3. Do one of the following:
- Scroll down the License app's Results area to the Avid Ada Transcribe license.
- Type "avid a" in the Filter Licenses field in the Results area toolbar.
4. Check the Current Quantity.
For Transcribe, the number on the left indicates how many minutes have already been consumed and the number on the right indicates the total number of minutes that can be transcribed within a cycle.
Adding Transcript Tab Items to a Sequence You can add items from the Transcript tab by simple drag and drop operations to a sequence. When you highlight text in a transcript or translation, the In and Out mark for the highlighted section are set on the timeline. By dragging from the Media Viewer you then add a segment to the sequence.
10 Using the Transcript Tab The term sequence is used generically throughout this topic to refer to an assembly of audio and video assets; it refers to any or all sequence types.
For more information about sequences, see "Understanding Sequence Types" on page 405 and "Working with Sequences" on page 421.
Note the following effects when highlighting text:
- To set In and Out marks in a transcript, you can highlight text within a segment, the entire segment
text or text across segments. You do not need to enable Edit mode.
- To set In and Out marks in a translation, you can highlight only an entire segment or several
segments; setting In and Out marks by highlighting text within a segment is not supported.
When you start highlighting text, In and Out marks are set on the timeline.
- While you highlight text, the In or Out mark moves on the time line: If you highlight text/segments
upwards, the In mark is moved, if you highlight text/segments downwards, the Out mark is moved.
To add Transcript tab sections to a sequence:
1. Open a master clip or subclip in the Asset Editor.
2. Click the Transcript tab.
3. Open the sequence you want to edit.
4. Click the Asset Editor Timeline button to show the Sequence Timeline.
5. Click the Asset Editor Source/Record Monitor toggle button to switch Asset Editor to Source mode.
6. To define a section in a transcript, do the following:
a. Select the transcript from the transcript selection control.
b. Highlight text with the mouse pointer: a text portion within a segment, the entire segment text, or text across segments.
The In and Out mark for the highlighted section are set on the timeline.
7. To define a section in a translation, do the following:
a. Click the Translation toggle button to switch to Translation mode.
b. (optional) Select another transcript from the transcript selection control.
c. Select the translation from the translation selection control.
d. Do one of the following:
- To mark an entire segment, double-click on the segment.
The In and Out mark for the segment are set on the timeline.
- To mark several segments, click on a segment text and then highlighting the segments
above or below with the mouse pointer.
The In of the first segment and the Out mark of the last the segment are set on the timeline.
8. Click in the Media Viewer, drag the image to the Sequence Timeline and drop it into the position you
10 Using the Transcript Tab want the segment to appear in the sequence.
11 Working with Graphics Management Systems 11 Working with Graphics Management Systems This topic describes the processes for working with a graphics management system that has been integrated with MediaCentral Cloud UX. In this chapter, Maestro News is used as an example of a graphics management system.
What is a Graphics Management System?
A graphics management system allows you to create and manage graphic elements that are often used in broadcast workflows. When you connect one of these systems to MediaCentral Cloud UX, you can enable custom graphic workflows that allow you to integrate your graphics with areas of the Cloud UX user interface such as the Browse app, Search app, Rundown app, or others.
The MediaCentral Platform is designed to enable easy integration of both Avid and non-Avid systems.
Graphics integration is a licensed feature. Before you can integrate any graphics management system with MediaCentral Cloud UX, your organization must purchase and apply a valid license using the MediaCentral Cloud UX Licensing app. For more information, see “Using the Licensing App” in the Avid MediaCentral | Cloud UX Installation Guide.
> **Note:** While Maestro News is used as an example graphics management system, the concepts described in
this document might or might not apply to other graphics systems. If you are not using Maestro News, see the documentation that came with your graphics management system for supplemental information and custom workflows.
Maestro News as a Graphics Management System Maestro News is a universal controller that lets you create and manage template-based, on-air 3D graphics. A Maestro user works with a data entry template (page), which is linked to a 3D graphic scene created in Maestro Designer. A graphic artist creates exports that are customized by adding controls to a template in the Maestro News PageEditor. These controls allow journalists to edit the graphic’s content during a broadcast.
The Maestro user can add graphics, media, and text to the template, update these data elements in real time, preview the graphic for broadcast, and send it to air. The integration of Maestro News with MediaCentral Cloud UX allows you to edit graphic templates through the Graphics tab of the Asset Editor, and add it to the MediaCentral Newsroom Management story and related sequence. The MediaCentral Newsroom Management rundown appears in the Maestro News controller and can be played to air.
For information on the various assets available in the Maestro database, see "Working with MediaCentral Newsroom Management Assets" on page 151.
Viewing Maestro News Assets You can locate Maestro News assets by navigating to them in the Browse app or by searching for them in the Search app. When viewed through the Browse app, you might see Templates, Workspace, and Media folders in the Maestro database as shown in the following illustration.
11 Working with Graphics Management Systems TEMPLATES: This folder contains Maestro News templates. You can load templates into the Asset
- Editor to customize them or drag them directly into a Newsroom Management rundown or associated
sequence.
- WORKSPACE: This folder contains Maestro News items. Items are template instances that have been
customized.
- MEDIA: (if applicable) This folder includes media assets that can be added to graphic elements in the
Graphics tab of the Asset Editor. This media lives on a shared storage system that is connected directly to the Maestro News system. For more information on configuring access to the Media directory, see the Avid Maestro | News Installation and Configuration Guide.
> **Note:** Depending on your Maestro News user rights, access to specific directories or templates might be
restricted. If you do not have a matching user account on both the MediaCentral Cloud UX and Maestro News systems, you might see the Maestro database appear in the Browse app, but you might not be able to navigate through the directory tree.
You can use the Search app to find Maestro News assets as you would with any other MediaCentral module. For more information, see "Searching for Assets" on page 154. The following processes describes how to find Maestro graphic elements using the Browse app, how to load them into the Asset Editor, and how to alter the display options in the user interface to show additional metadata.
To load a graphic element into the Asset Editor using the Browse app:
1. Click the Browse app button in the Fast Bar at the top of the user interface.
2. Locate the Maestro database in the directory tree of the Browse app sidebar and click on the arrow
to the left of the module to expand the first level of the database.
3. Navigate to an asset in either the Templates or Workspace folder or sub-folders.
4. Double-click on a template or item to load it into the Asset Editor and click on the Graphics tab of the
Asset Editor. For more information, see "Understanding the Graphics Tab" on the next page.
> **Note:** You cannot load assets from the Media directory into the Asset Editor in this release. Although you can load items into the Asset Editor, you cannot make changes to the item. If you need to
update an item, you must load the item into the Asset Editor through the associated story in the Rundown app.
11 Working with Graphics Management Systems To alter the Maestro News display options:
1. In either the Browse or Search apps, click the Display Options menu in the Results toolbar
A menu of additional metadata values appears, including a category for Maestro News attributes.
2. Click the arrow to the left of the Maestro News menu item to reveal the Maestro News options.
3. Click the box to the left of any of the following attributes to add or remove them from the Results
area of the Browse or Search app:
  - Channel: When you add an item to the rundown, it is assigned an output channel. For more
information, see “Assigning a Channel” in the Avid Maestro | News User Guide.
  - Duration (Browse only): Graphic duration in the following format: [hours]:[minutes]:[seconds]:
[frames] @[framerate] [interlaced/non-interlaced]
  - For example: 00:00:02:08 @25i.
  - Media Path: Displays the file name and file path of images and clips used that are registered in
the Maestro News database. By default, assets are located in MEDIA folder.
  - UniqueID: An unique identifier of a template or item.
  - VSlot: Virtual Slots are visual layers in a channel (preview or on-air). For more information
about Virtual Slots, see “Virtual Slots” in the Avid Maestro | News User Guide.
4. You can close the Display Options menu by taking one of the following actions:
- Click outside the menu to save your changes.
- Press Esc to close the menu without making any changes.
Understanding the Graphics Tab MediaCentral Cloud UX systems that are licensed for a graphics workflow include an additional tab in the Asset Editor labeled: Graphics. When you load a graphic element (template or item) into the Asset Editor, this tab includes the controls that you can use to customize the graphic. Templates are created in the Maestro Page Editor with predefined controls. The scope of changes that you are allowed to make to the element are defined by these controls (which can vary from template to template). For more information on creating templates, see the Maestro News documentation.
In addition to the Graphics tab, a preview of the graphic is displayed in the source monitor of the Media Viewer. For more information, see "Previewing Graphic Elements" on page 403.
> **Note:** If you do not see the Graphics tab, ask your MediaCentral Cloud UX Administrator to verify that your
user account is associated with either an Edit client license and that the required entitlements are applied to your account through the User Management Administrator app.
11 Working with Graphics Management Systems The following illustration and table describe the different areas of the Graphics tab.
Section Description 1 Graphics The toolbar includes controls such as Drag, Save, and Render. For more information, Toolbar see "The Graphics Toolbar" below.
